definition:
κλίνω 
 [in LXX chiefly for נָטָה ;] 
  1. to make to bend, to bow: τ. κεφαλήν (of one dying), Jhn.19:30; τ. πρόσωτον (of terrified persons), Luk.24:5. 
   2. to make to lean, to rest: τ. κεφαλήν (in sleep), Mat.8:20, Luk.9:58. 
   3. As in Hom., of soldiers, to turn (to flight), παρεμβολάς, Heb.11:34. 
   4. In late Gk. (as mid, in cl.), to decline: intrans., ἡ ἡμέρα, Luk.9:12 24:29.†
 (AS)
